title: rubus erraticus
intro: Rubus erraticus, also known as the wild raspberry or wood raspberry, is a perennial shrub that belongs to the rose family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Rubus erraticus include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and even support immune function, making it a popular choice for natural remedies. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve making teas, infusions, or tinctures from the leaves, stems, and fruits, which can be used to address various health issues, from skin conditions to gastrointestinal problems.
